Hey guys, JB here, the Wolf is in the house for your daily motivation slash skills training. I want to talk about standards. It's such a crucial topic and I'm still working on here and I have a thought fresh in my mind and that is good enough is what? That's having a good standard. You know having an excellent stand, it means you want to be excellent at that level above there, extraordinary. And again, you know, what I learned from Baylor and I always cite Baylor when I go through the intergame. I really crystallized a lot of the things that I had already known because again, while I knew this stuff intuitively and it taught it to the Stratonites, right? And other people over the years, NLP was what sort of made it more formulaic for me because it raised my own awareness to what I was doing so effectively. In fact, as the story goes, when I went to see Baylor many years back, you know, when I was trying to dissect my own strategy, why was I able to take any salesperson and turn them into a world class? I understood the straight line system. Yeah, that was powerful, right? That was a winning formula that allowed people to transfer certainty and close deals. But it was more than that. You know, I had these kids that would come to me and they were young, uneducated, disempowered, had never been told by their parents. They were capable of greatness. Any greatness they had, intermittent and beaten out of them, really, you know? So they were disempowered, average people with low standards, yet they walk into my boardroom and a few weeks later, they would look like different people, the way they walked, the way they talked, where they carried themselves, how they dressed, everything. And they say, well, okay, Jordan, I get that, but you know, you taught these guys to make millions of dollars a year. You gave me that much money. I would dress and look and act better. And while that's true, it doesn't apply here because the Stratton training program was four months long. You get it? So when a kid came to me, he wasn't making money for four months, but the change was almost instant. Why? Because there were things I was saying to everybody about acting as if, raising your standard, leading with your body, leading with the way you speak. In other words, even though the money wasn't made yet, they already appeared to be wealthy. They were already acting, living, and not maybe spending the money, but they were applying, carrying themselves like winners. At the end of the day, that aspect, that inner game aspect, is what merges with the outer game and bam, creates this short-fire mass of success. And again, in the short term, the indicator is state management. Can you access your skills in the moment? Point being is that while state management is your in-the-moment indicator for wealth and success, can you access the skills you have? Your standard is your long-term indicator, where you'll end up. High standard, you end up in the right place. Low standard, you're not going to end up doing well in life. In the certification course, I really go through in detail, how you raise your standard for all aspects of your life. But I've also gone through it less formally in some of the intensives. The point is though, is our state is the in-the-moment indicator. Our standard is the long-term barometer that guides, dictates, and ensures our success. Not a mind. Make sense? All right, I love you all. Remember, if that standard you have is too low, raise your standard. Don't go through life living it like a 10-watt life bulb. Seriously, burn bright. Make each day count. Be the best you can be. I promise you, once you get into the habit of that, it sounds like a habit, right? I'm just being the best you can be. Life is so much sweeter. It smells sweeter. It tastes sweeter. It is sweeter. That's where the beauty of life is at that high level. Love you all. Talk again tomorrow.
